findings.claim: Key option A is the longest option and the only one that both names the true cause and explicitly rejects two traps ('rather than its age or tyres'), a mild completeness/length cue a test-wise student could lean on.
findings.evidence: A (23 words): 'The grit and salt that traffic drags over the line, together with snowplough blades, rather than its age or tyres running over it.' vs B(19)/C(18)/D(18). A is the only single-barrelled-plus-negation option.
findings.why: Does not flip the SOUND verdict — the passage still supports only A and the distractors remain genuine misconceptions — but the length/dual-content asymmetry is a real minor psychometric observation worth recording.
